content: {"notes": [{"url": "https://jamanetwork.com/journals/jama/article-abstract/2768095", "title": "Limited evidence for the health effects and safety of intermittent fasting among patients with type 2 diabetes", "summary": "Caloric restriction and weight loss beneficially influence the development and progression of type 2 diabetes. Weight loss in patients with type 2 diabetes, whether through surgical or dietary interventions, leads to improvement in glycemic control, hypertension, abnormal lipid levels, and nonalcoholic fatty liver disease.1 Caloric restriction is an effective method for weight loss when followed consistently over time. Research suggests that intermittent fasting may also influence type 2 diabetes.", "perspectives": ["Scientific"], "key_facts": ["Weight loss in patients with type 2 diabetes, whether through surgical or dietary interventions, leads to improvement in glycemic control, hypertension, abnormal lipid levels, and nonalcoholic fatty liver disease.1 Caloric restriction is an effective"], "knowledge_contribution": "Evidence: Weight loss in patients with type 2 diabetes, whether through surgical or dietary interventions, leads to improvement in"}, {"url": "https://www.mdpi.com/2076-328x/7/1/4", "title": "Potential benefits and harms of intermittent energy restriction and intermittent fasting amongst obese, overweight and normal weight subjects\u2014a narrative\u00a0\u2026", "summary": "Potential Benefits and Harms of Intermittent Energy Restriction and Intermittent Fasting Amongst Obese, Overweight and Normal Weight Subjects\u2014A Narrative Review of Human and Animal Evidence Abstract 1. Is IER Associated with Greater Weight Control than CER? 3.1.1. Weight Loss amongst People with Overweight or Obesity 3.1.2.", "perspectives": ["Scientific"], "key_facts": ["Is IER Associated with Greater Weight Control than CER? 3.1.1.", "Weight Loss amongst People with Overweight or Obesity 3.1.2.", "Adherence to IER and CER amongst People with Overweight or Obesity 3.1.3.", "Maintaining Weight Loss amongst People with Overweight or Obesity 3.2.", "Adipose Stores and Adipocyte Size 4.1.1."], "knowledge_contribution": "Evidence: Is IER Associated with Greater Weight Control than CER? 3.1.1."}, {"url": "https://karger.com/mip/article-abstract/34/1/142/909949", "title": "Health benefits of intermittent fasting", "summary": "Abstract Summary: We propose that intermittent fasting (time-restricted eating), in agreement with the conclusions of other biologists, as revealed in recent publications, promotes the achievement of numerous health benefits including the extension of human and animal lifespans. Background: There is evidence, obtained both with animal model systems and with humans, that intermittent fasting has health benefits. These benefits include extended longevity, weight loss, and counteracting various disease conditions.", "perspectives": ["Scientific"], "key_facts": ["IF is an umbrella term, used for several calorie-restricting fasting regimes: the 5:2 diet where one fasts for 2 days a week and eats normally during the other days; the 16:8 plan where one eats within an 8-h window and fasts for 16 h; the alternate-", "In a majority of reported cases, these fasting regimes have been shown to be safe: several clinical studies have reported significant metabolic benefits with no or only very mild side effects.", "The notion that fasting is good for health also has a long history since the publication of the book \u201cFasting for the Cure of Disease\u201d in 1908 by Linda B.", "IF regimes have been reported to lower fasting insulin concentrations [31\u201233] and HbA1c (glycated hemoglobin) [34\u201237] in otherwise healthy individuals with obesity and/or prediabetes.", "An overwhelming majority of relevant published studies have reported that fasting regimes result in anywhere between 1.3 and 84% loss of body weight with improvement of metabolic health [32, 33, 38\u201242]."], "knowledge_contribution": "Evidence: IF is an umbrella term, used for several calorie-restricting fasting regimes: the 5:2 diet where one fasts for 2 days a "}, {"url": "https://www.jandonline.org/article/S2212-2672(15)00205-1/abstract", "title": "Intermittent fasting and human metabolic health", "summary": "", "perspectives": ["Scientific"], "key_facts": [], "knowledge_contribution": "Background."}, {"url": "https://www.cambridge.org/core/journals/proceedings-of-the-nutrition-society/article/effects-of-intermittent-fasting-on-glucose-and-lipid-metabolism/8803CC1517F53CEF2BF8BFDC06A816D6", "title": "Effects of intermittent fasting on glucose and lipid metabolism", "summary": "We are currently in the midst of a global obesity epidemic, with its prevalence more than doubling since 1980( 1 ). The development of obesity is closely associated with a number of metabolic complications including insulin resistance and dyslipidaemia, which can in turn increase an individual's risk of developing type 2 diabetes mellitus (T2DM) and CVD( Reference Leiter, Fitchett and Gilbert 2 ). Glucose and lipid homeostasis can be improved through modest weight-loss, which due to current accepted dietary advice, is most commonly achieved via a modest continuous (daily) energy restriction (CER)( 3 ).", "perspectives": ["Scientific"], "key_facts": ["We are currently in the midst of a global obesity epidemic, with its prevalence more than doubling since 1980( 1 ).", "With these dietary strategies, the pattern of energy restriction and/or timing of food intake are altered, so that individuals undergo frequently repeated periods of fasting (Table 1).", "Their basic premise compared to CER is that an individual may not need to energy restrict either every day, or at all, to attain metabolic benefits.", "This is often referred to as modified fasting, such that, the term fasting in this IER context denotes periods of severe (total or partial) energy restriction.", "There are also some data demonstrating the successful applications of IER as a weight-maintenance strategy following weight-loss for periods of up to 1 year( Reference Harvie, Wright and Pegington 37 , Reference Wegman, Guo and Bennion 39 )."], "knowledge_contribution": "Evidence: We are currently in the midst of a global obesity epidemic, with its prevalence more than doubling since 1980( 1 )."}, {"url": "https://www.jandonline.org/article/S2212-2672(15)00205-1/abstract", "title": "Intermittent fasting and human metabolic health", "summary": "", "perspectives": ["Scientific"], "key_facts": [], "knowledge_contribution": "Background."}]}
